horizons.label: 0–6 months
horizons.objective: Convert thesis into a defensible, fundable Canadian platform.
milestones: Incorporate Canadian entity (federal + provincial registrations).
Hire country lead, head of deployment, and head of regulatory.
Sign 2 utility cooperation MOUs (target ON + QC or BC).
Identify 3 pilot sites: 2 commercial DCFC, 1 fleet depot.
Compliance map: CSA, Measurement Canada, ZEVIP eligibility, OCPP/ISO 15118.
Finalize go/no-go memo for Phase 2 (pilot deployment).
horizons.decisionGate: Approve Phase 2 pilot capex only if: 2 utility MOUs signed, 3 sites cleared, ZEVIP path identified.

horizons.label: 6–24 months
horizons.objective: Prove unit economics and build operational muscle.
milestones: Energize 2 DCFC pilots and 1 BESS-buffered fleet depot.
Launch home charging program via 1–2 dealer/retail partners.
Submit ZEVIP and provincial funding applications.
Software platform (CPMS + telemetry + billing) live in Canadian cloud region.
Reach 10–15 sites under construction or operating by month 24.
Begin packaging O&M/software for third-party site hosts.
horizons.decisionGate: Proceed to expansion phase only if: 1) blended utilization ≥ pro-forma base, 2) at least 1 funding award secured, 3) zero serious safety/compliance incidents.

horizons.label: 2–7 years
horizons.objective: Become a credible Canadian operator across charging, storage, and software.
milestones: Operate 50–100 charging sites across ON/QC/BC, with 8–12 fleet depots.
BESS attached at majority of new sites; participate in capacity/ancillary markets where allowed.
Software/O&M generates ≥25% of recurring revenue.
Standardized partnership template with Canadian utilities and fleet operators.
Optional: selective commercial vehicle pilots, only if tariff/policy environment changes.
horizons.decisionGate: Decision on local assembly / JV: only if expansion phase EBITDA-positive in base case and policy environment stable.

horizons.label: 7–20 years
horizons.objective: Earn the right to be treated as a Canadian new-energy company.
milestones: Local assembly of charging hardware and/or BESS modules.
Strategic JV or minority investment with Canadian fleet, utility, or industrial partner.
Long-duration storage and grid services as a stand-alone business line.
Optional: local-assembly or licensed-model vehicle participation, decided on policy + economics, not on initial thesis.
Canadian R&D footprint: charging power electronics, BMS, software.
horizons.decisionGate: Continuous review: every 24 months, reassess thesis vs. policy, capital cost, and competitive structure.
